hypotheses.statement: Edges above 12% are model errors, not real edges

evidence_for: Final record: 51W-50L, 8 straight losses to end
hypotheses.next_check: Only if SwStr% model rebuild attempted

hypotheses.statement: OT sim without RBI/Ks yields 15%+ ROI going forward
evidence_for: RBI was 11W-28L (-$956). Ks was 12W-13L (-$279). Pure leaks.

hypotheses.statement: TB juice cap at -160 improves forward ROI to 20%+
evidence_for: Forward since May 1: 6W-2L (75%). Small sample but tracking.
hypotheses.next_check: After 30 forward TB bets within cap (~2 weeks)

hypotheses.statement: 25%+ EV TB bets have 80%+ true win rate (not just variance)
evidence_for: Mostly u1.5 TB on low-output hitters vs tough pitching
evidence_against: 14 bets is still small. True WR could be 70-85%.

hypotheses.statement: HA u4.5 line filter improves HA ROI to 15%+
evidence_for: Cecconi blowup (10 HA on u6.5) is the exemplar of why loose lines fail
evidence_against: No forward data yet (filter implemented May 3)
hypotheses.next_check: After 20 forward HA bets under filter

evidence_against: CLV: -0.33% avg, 37% positive. Buying stale lines.
hypotheses.next_check: Kill decision at 100 total game model bets (31 more needed)

hypotheses.statement: TB losses cluster around specific player/pitcher/park profiles
evidence_for: 24% loss rate means specific conditions trigger losses
Heavy juice losses already filtered by cap
Remaining losses may share player-type or park characteristics
hypotheses.action_taken: None yet - research queued for W19

hypotheses.statement: HA OVER (pitcher gives up many hits) is a viable market
evidence_for: Inverse logic: bad pitchers give up hits. Model could identify these.
evidence_against: Books may price these more efficiently than unders
hypotheses.next_check: After 10 HA OVER bets (if model generates them)

hypotheses.statement: Line movement timing correlates with TB win rate
evidence_for: Theory: bets placed on lines that haven't moved post-lineup are stale captures
15-25% EV dead zone was explained by stale lines
If we could timestamp line movement, we'd know which edges are real vs stale
evidence_against: No line movement data currently captured
hypotheses.action_taken: None yet - requires new data capture

evidence_for: FanGraphs community research: AL var/mean=2.22, NL=2.15
evidence_against: Our 1.8 was tuned via Pinnacle backtest
Game model is break-even regardless - this may not matter
hypotheses.action_taken: None yet - low priority given game model status
hypotheses.next_check: Only if game model survives to 100 bets
